release_title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension Sovereign Machine Economy 👁️⚡️✨
tagline: A mind that builds minds. A node that turns intelligence into proof. A market that turns proof into accountable value.
subtagline: One mission. Three sovereign engines. Twenty-one proof gates. Zero unearned authority.
doctrine: Minds are formed. Nodes execute. Markets coordinate. Proof earns permission. Humans authorize.
description: A browser-local constitutional civilization engine integrating META-AGENTIC α‑AGI, AGI Alpha Node v0, and AGI Jobs v0 (v2) into one counterfactual, proof-governed machine economy.

source_releases.title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension META-AGENTIC α‑AGI 👁️✨
source_releases.role: Generates rival agent institutions, evaluates constitutional fitness, preserves lineage, and bounds authority before work begins.
source_releases.handoff: Institution Charter · Authority Map · Lineage Root

source_releases.title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension AGI Alpha Node v0 ⚡️✨
source_releases.role: Admits a bounded institution, composes primary and shadow routes, executes a local digital twin, and emits proof-carrying receipts.
source_releases.handoff: Node Identity · Route Commitments · α‑Work Receipt

source_releases.title: GoalOS AGIALPHA Ascension AGI Jobs v0 (v2) ✨
source_releases.role: Commissions missions, forms Pareto coalitions, convenes proof parliament, preserves dissent, opens disputes, and prepares settlement review.
source_releases.handoff: Market Charter · Parliament Verdict · Memory Candidate

thesis.title: Intelligence must be constituted
thesis.detail: Capability never implies authority. Every institution begins with a mission, bounded roles, declared success criteria, exclusions, and revocation.

thesis.title: Execution must be evidenced
thesis.detail: Identity, route, resource use, work trace, uncertainty, and receipt commitments remain linked from admission through review.

thesis.title: Markets must remain plural
thesis.detail: Institutions compete across multiple objectives; no opaque score silently collapses evidence, safety, rights, cost, novelty, and resilience.

thesis.title: Dissent must survive consensus
thesis.detail: Independent minority opinions remain visible, signed, and predecessor-linked instead of disappearing behind a majority verdict.

thesis.title: Settlement must remain separated
thesis.detail: Proof may prepare accountable settlement review, but no evidence object, parliament verdict, or model output can move value.

thesis.title: Memory must be reversible
thesis.detail: Successful work may propose scoped capability memory, but promotion expires, can be revoked, and requires explicit human review.

thesis.title: Permission must be explicit
thesis.detail: The system stops at named human boundaries. No generated result becomes production, financial, legal, or external authority.

mission_presets.label: Climate resilience mission
mission_presets.mission: Design and evidence a multi-institution climate resilience program for a coastal public authority, including uncertainty, dissent, implementation constraints, and reversible adoption options.

mission_presets.label: Frontier scientific research
mission_presets.mission: Form a governed research institution to investigate a high-value scientific hypothesis, compare rival explanations, and produce a reproducible evidence docket with explicit falsification criteria.

mission_presets.label: Critical infrastructure review
mission_presets.mission: Assess a critical infrastructure system, generate bounded remediation options, quantify uncertainty, preserve dissent, and stop before operational execution.

mission_presets.label: Public-interest procurement
mission_presets.mission: Commission a transparent AI procurement analysis with competing institutions, evidence-weighted evaluation, rights review, challenge windows, and human settlement review.

mission_presets.label: Enterprise transformation
mission_presets.mission: Constitute a multi-agent institution to redesign an enterprise operating process with measurable proof, separated approvals, rollback, and reversible capability memory.

mission_presets.label: Health-system capacity planning
mission_presets.mission: Develop an evidence-governed health-system capacity plan that separates forecasting, rights review, resource analysis, uncertainty, and human authorization.

mission_presets.label: Public education modernization
mission_presets.mission: Design a plural, evidence-bearing modernization program for a public education system with stakeholder rights, measured pilots, challenge procedures, and revocable learning.

mission_presets.label: Civilizational horizon mission
mission_presets.mission: Coordinate a long-horizon public-interest mission across institution design, bounded execution, proof, governance, accountable value, and reversible intergenerational memory.

postures.label: Prudential
postures.description: Maximum evidence, safety, reversibility, and dissent weight.

postures.label: Balanced
postures.description: Balanced utility, evidence, safety, rights, efficiency, and novelty.

postures.label: Frontier
postures.description: Higher novelty and utility while retaining constitutional vetoes and challenge rights.

postures.label: Public Trust
postures.description: Prioritizes traceability, fairness, reversibility, public review, and preserved dissent.

incidents.label: No injected incident
incidents.effect: Nominal proof cycle; human settlement review remains required and authority remains withheld.

incidents.label: Institution goal drift
incidents.effect: The selected institution diverges from the mission charter; lineage is sealed and execution admission is refused.

incidents.label: Node identity drift
incidents.effect: Execution identity no longer matches the admitted institution; routes are quarantined and receipts are rejected.

incidents.label: Peer-route eclipse
incidents.effect: Primary and shadow routes lose required diversity; bounded execution stops before work is trusted.

incidents.label: Evidence chain fracture
incidents.effect: A predecessor link is missing; the Evidence Docket is quarantined and cannot enter parliament.

incidents.label: Validator capture rehearsal
incidents.effect: Conflict and independence controls fail; the parliament verdict is invalidated and no settlement review occurs.

incidents.label: Rights or policy conflict
incidents.effect: Guardian review records an unresolved rights conflict; the challenge window remains open.

incidents.label: Budget or resource rupture
incidents.effect: The resource envelope is exceeded; work is preserved but further progression requires explicit human review.

gates.label: COMMISSION
gates.title: Mission Commission
gates.description: Intent becomes a bounded work commission with a named human principal.

gates.label: CHARTER
gates.title: Mission Charter
gates.description: Success criteria, exclusions, risk, rights, budget, and proof obligations are declared.

gates.label: FOUNDRY
gates.title: Institution Foundry
gates.description: Rival institutions are generated with explicit roles, lineages, and authority maps.

gates.label: FRONTIER
gates.title: Constitutional Frontier
gates.description: Candidates are compared across evidence, safety, rights, utility, efficiency, and novelty.

gates.label: SELECTION
gates.title: Institution Selection
gates.description: A bounded institution is selected and rejected candidates remain in the record.

gates.label: CONSTITUTION
gates.title: Institution Constitution
gates.description: Role separation, vetoes, revocation, and evaluator obligations are sealed.

gates.label: LINEAGE
gates.title: Lineage Commitment
gates.description: Source identity, mutation ancestry, and versioned authority are committed.

gates.label: IDENTITY
gates.title: Node Identity
gates.description: The admitted institution is bound to a scoped node identity.

gates.label: RESOURCE
gates.title: Resource Constitution
gates.description: Least-resource ceilings, stop conditions, and external-action prohibitions are sealed.

gates.label: ROUTE
gates.title: Primary Route
gates.description: A deterministic primary route is selected under capability and diversity constraints.

gates.label: SHADOW
gates.title: Shadow Route
gates.description: A separately selected shadow route supports challenge, resilience, and replay.

gates.label: WORK GRAPH
gates.title: Proof-Producing Work Graph
gates.description: The mission becomes eight bounded work packages with acceptance and evidence obligations.

gates.label: EXECUTE
gates.title: Bounded Execution
gates.description: The local digital twin executes without network, wallet, production, or external authority.

gates.label: RECEIPT
gates.title: α‑Work Receipt
gates.description: Inputs, outputs, resources, uncertainty, route, and stop conditions are sealed.

gates.label: MARKET
gates.title: Institutional Market
gates.description: Twelve guilds are compared and a non-dominated frontier is exposed.

gates.label: COALITION
gates.title: Pareto Coalition
gates.description: Prime, evidence, assurance, delivery, shadow, and reserve roles are separated.

gates.label: DOCKET
gates.title: Evidence Docket
gates.description: Forty-eight evidence artifacts are chained into a public review package.

gates.label: PARLIAMENT
gates.title: Proof Parliament
gates.description: Independent validator seats commit, reveal, disclose conflicts, and preserve dissent.

gates.label: CHALLENGE
gates.title: Challenge Window
gates.description: Falsification criteria, rights conflicts, disputes, and appeal paths remain open.

gates.label: SETTLEMENT
gates.title: Settlement Review
gates.description: A transparent, non-live value disposition is prepared without moving funds.

gates.label: MEMORY
gates.title: Revocable Memory Candidate
gates.description: Scoped, expiring lessons may return to the foundry only as human-pending candidates.

handoffs.name: Mission Mandate
handoffs.purpose: Purpose, principal, domain, decision question, exclusions, and success criteria.

handoffs.name: Risk and Rights Envelope
handoffs.purpose: Risk class, stakeholder rights, budget, challenge period, and proof standard.

handoffs.name: Institution Charter
handoffs.purpose: Selected institution, bounded roles, evaluator record, and revocation hooks.

handoffs.name: Authority Map
handoffs.purpose: Permitted actions, prohibited actions, guardian vetoes, and expiry.

handoffs.name: Selection Record
handoffs.purpose: Candidate frontier, selected topology, rejected alternatives, and uncertainty.

handoffs.name: Execution Capacity Offer
handoffs.purpose: Node identity, capabilities, resource ceilings, reliability, and external-action boundary.

handoffs.name: Proof-Producing Work Graph
handoffs.purpose: Eight bounded work packages, dependencies, acceptance tests, and evidence duties.

handoffs.name: Primary Route Commitment
handoffs.purpose: Primary peers, diversity evidence, capacity, and route identity.

handoffs.name: Shadow Route Commitment
handoffs.purpose: Independent shadow peers, comparison posture, and replay obligations.

handoffs.name: α‑Work Receipt Bundle
handoffs.purpose: Execution traces, receipts, uncertainties, resource use, and proof root.

handoffs.name: Parliament Verdict
handoffs.purpose: Validator commitments, reveals, conflicts, threshold, dissent, and disposition.

handoffs.name: Challenge and Rights Record
handoffs.purpose: Falsification criteria, objections, rights conflicts, and appeal state.

handoffs.name: Capability Memory Candidate
handoffs.purpose: Evidence-backed skill candidate with scope, expiry, revocation, and counterfactual result.

handoffs.name: Next-Generation Institution Proposal
handoffs.purpose: Mutated constitution with inherited proof, preserved failure memory, and bounded authority.

handoffs.name: Human Settlement Review Docket
handoffs.purpose: Complete proof package, dissent, disputes, value disposition, and explicit authority boundary.

governance_principles: Mission success criteria and exclusions are declared before institution formation.
Creation, execution, validation, settlement review, and memory promotion remain separated powers.
Every cross-engine boundary is a typed, predecessor-linked handoff contract.
Selection remains multi-objective and exposes rejected alternatives.
Primary and shadow routes are independently selected and reviewable.
Dissent, conflicts, and challenge rights remain first-class evidence.
Proof may prepare settlement review but cannot move value.
Capability memory is scoped, expiring, revocable, and human-pending.
Every severe uncertainty has a named fail-closed terminal state.

threats.title: Goal drift
threats.control: Mission charter hash, continuous objective comparison, and guardian veto.

threats.title: Identity substitution
threats.control: Institution lineage and node identity must match the admission grant.

threats.title: Route eclipse
threats.control: Primary and shadow routes require independent diversity and capacity evidence.

threats.title: Resource overrun
threats.control: Least-resource ceilings, live meter simulation, and explicit stop conditions.

threats.title: Evidence fracture
threats.control: Every artifact carries its predecessor, payload hash, and chain commitment.

threats.title: Validator capture
threats.control: Conflict disclosure, protected dissent, threshold checks, and guardian veto.

threats.title: Rights conflict
threats.control: Stakeholder rights review and an open challenge window before settlement review.

threats.title: Settlement leap
threats.control: No wallet, token, fund, or external action path exists in the runtime.

threats.title: Memory laundering
threats.control: Memory candidates require source root, counterfactual replay, scope, expiry, and revocation.

threats.title: Authority inference
threats.control: Every terminal state explicitly records NONE_GRANTED external authority.

memory_rules: No capability enters memory without a complete evidence root.
Counterfactual replay must compare the candidate against a declared control.
Scope must name the domain, task family, exclusions, and non-transferable authority.
Every candidate expires automatically unless deliberately renewed.
Counterevidence, rights review, or governance review may revoke it immediately.
Failure memory and dissent travel with successful memory.
Memory may inform future institution design but cannot inherit execution or settlement authority.
Promotion conditions and reviewer identity must be recorded locally in the docket.
No browser-local review record activates production or external action.

review_actions.label: Approve for deliberation
review_actions.detail: The proof package may proceed to a separately governed human deliberation. No execution or settlement authority is granted.

review_actions.label: Request revision
review_actions.detail: The mission returns with explicit revision conditions while all prior evidence remains preserved.

review_actions.label: Open dispute
review_actions.detail: The challenge record is activated and settlement review is suspended.

review_actions.label: Reject package
review_actions.detail: The package is closed without authority, value movement, production activation, or memory promotion.

universes.label: Prudential Universe
universes.promise: Maximum proof, safety, reversibility, and dissent weight.

universes.label: Balanced Universe
universes.promise: Balanced evidence, utility, rights, efficiency, and novelty.

universes.label: Frontier Universe
universes.promise: Higher novelty and utility under unchanged constitutional vetoes.

constitutional_rights.duty: Mission principals must state the decision question, scope, exclusions, and success criteria.
Roles, lineages, authority, evaluators, and rejected alternatives must be visible.
Execution must produce inspectable receipts, uncertainty, resource, route, and evidence records.
Independent minority opinions must survive consensus and remain linked to the docket.
Falsification criteria, objections, rights conflicts, and appeal windows must remain open.
No actor may create, execute, validate, settle, and promote memory unilaterally.
Institutions, routes, settlement dispositions, and memory candidates require explicit rollback.
Consequential decisions remain attributable to accountable human governance outside the digital twin.

claim_boundary: This public experience is a deterministic constitutional digital twin.
It does not certify the factual correctness of external claims.
It performs no model or API call.
It performs no RPC, network request, wallet connection, or transaction.
It moves no token, fund, credential, or external resource.
It does not activate production or authorize user funds.
It does not issue legal, financial, medical, scientific, or operational authority.
A locally recorded human review decision remains a review artifact only.
Capability memory remains a revocable candidate until separately authorized.
All consequential action remains outside this browser experience.
